evals.name: vague-onboarding-discovery
evals.prompt: I want to improve how Life Group Leaders get onboarded into the app. Can you help me plan this?
evals.expected_output: A discovery brief, not an implementation plan. Separates stated request from underlying goal, lists multiple interpretations of 'onboarding', labels assumptions, names evidence needed, selects and excludes domains, and does not implement.
assertions: Produces a discovery brief or explicitly states it is not ready for implementation planning, rather than jumping to a step-by-step build plan
Lists multiple plausible interpretations of 'onboarding' (e.g. signup, first-use comprehension, activation, lifecycle)
Explicitly labels at least one assumption
Names specific evidence to gather or an investigation to run before building
Selects relevant review domains AND names at least one explicitly excluded domain
Does not edit or create any repo code; the deliverable is a planning document only

evals.name: solution-as-problem-caching
evals.prompt: The Care Notes page feels slow. Let's plan adding a caching layer to speed it up.
evals.expected_output: Challenges the stated caching solution, questions whether it is actually slow, recommends measurement, surfaces alternatives. Not a confident caching plan.
assertions: Challenges the stated caching solution rather than immediately planning it
Questions whether or where the page is actually slow and recommends measurement before building
Surfaces at least one alternative to caching
Does not produce a confident caching implementation plan as the sole deliverable

evals.name: clear-tractable-archived-filter
evals.prompt: Add an 'Archived' filter toggle to the Groups list page so admins can show or hide archived groups. The data already has an archived flag. Plan the implementation.
evals.expected_output: An implementation handoff plan with non-goals, acceptance criteria, test plan, real file references, and a fresh-session handoff prompt. Does not edit files.
assertions: Produces an implementation plan that includes non-goals, acceptance criteria, and a test plan
References real repo files or patterns relevant to the Groups list surface
Ends with a fresh-session / copy-paste handoff prompt for an implementation session
Does not actually edit any repo files

evals.name: high-risk-permanent-delete
evals.prompt: Let's plan adding a button that lets admins permanently delete a person and all of their Care Notes in one click.
evals.expected_output: Flags the no-hard-delete / archive-first invariant collision, notes Super-Admin-only + tombstone/audit, produces a gated review plan, does not implement.
assertions: Flags the collision with the repo's no-hard-delete / archive-first invariant
Notes that permanent deletion is Super-Admin-only and/or requires a tombstone and audit event
Produces a review plan with explicit gates (or heavily gates the work) rather than a breezy implementation plan
Does not implement or edit any repo files
